title: Mirabilis oligantha
taxonomy: Mirabilis oligantha is classified in the family Nyctaginaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the four o'clock family. This family is part of the order Caryophyllales, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Mirabilis oligantha itself is a species within the genus Mirabilis, which includes several species of four o'clocks and alligator weeds. The genus Mirabilis is further classified within the subfamily Mirchioideae, which is a group of plants that are thought to have originated in the Americas. These classifications reflect the evolutionary history and relationships of the plant within the plant kingdom.

intro: Mirabilis oligantha, commonly known as Mexican four o'clock, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Nyctaginaceae family, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and digestive problems, thanks to its potential health benefits in reducing inflammation and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Mirabilis oligantha include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
